metadata.description: Essential Chinese vocabulary for school life, academic subjects, and study-related activities

content.name: talk
content.side_e: lǎoshī zài gēn xuésheng shuō huà.
content.side_f: The teacher is talking with the students.
content.side_g: 说话 is a separable verb-object compound (V+O); you cannot say 说话他, and objects/durations split it, e.g., 说了半天话. For 'say something specific,' use 说 + content (说中文), not 说话.

content.name: previous_one
content.side_e: shàng ge xīngqī wǒ hěn máng.
content.side_f: Last week I was very busy. (lit. 'previous-CL week')
content.side_g: 上个 pairs with time/sequence nouns: 上个月, 上个星期, 上个学期. Contrast with 下个 (next). For 'yesterday' use 昨天, not 上个天.

content.name: structural_particle
content.side_e: tā mànmàn de xiě zì.
content.side_f: He writes characters slowly.
content.side_g: This 地 is the adverbial particle attached after adjectives/reduplicated adjectives to modify verbs (慢慢地, 高兴地). Distinguish from 的 (attributive, before nouns) and 得 (after verbs, degree complement); all three are toneless 'de' but have different functions.

content.name: review
content.side_e: wǒ měitiān wǎnshang fùxí kèwén.
content.side_f: I review the text every evening.
content.side_g: 复习 means reviewing material already learned; pair with 预习 (preview, before class). Common collocations: 复习课文, 复习语法, 复习功课. The 复 here means 'again,' not 'reply.'

content.name: write
content.side_e: qǐng nǐ xiě yí ge zì.
content.side_f: Please write one character.
content.side_g: 写 takes direct objects (写字, 写信, 写名字). For handwriting practice use 写汉字; for 'to write to someone' use 给 + person + 写信. Often appears with 会 (会写) for ability.

content.name: character
content.side_e: zhè ge zì hěn nán xiě.
content.side_f: This character is hard to write.
content.side_g: 字 refers to individual written characters; 词 refers to words (may be one or more 字). Measure word is 个: 一个字. Contrast 汉字 (Chinese character) vs. 生字 (new character).

content.name: slow
content.side_e: tā shuō huà shuō de hěn màn.
content.side_f: She speaks very slowly.
content.side_g: 慢 is the antonym of 快. In V得 complements (说得很慢) it describes manner. Reduplicated 慢慢(地) functions adverbially: 慢慢来 ('take your time'). Imperative 慢点儿! means 'slow down.'

content.name: measure_pen
content.side_a: (measure word for long, thin, inflexible objects such as pens, rifles, etc.)
content.side_e: wǒ yǒu liǎng zhī bǐ.
content.side_f: I have two pens. (lit. 'two CL pen')
content.side_g: 支 is the measure word for long, thin, rigid items: pens, cigarettes, rifles, candles, flowers (stems). Also used for songs/tunes (一支歌). Do not use 个 with 笔 in careful speech.

content.name: pen
content.side_e: zhè zhī bǐ shì lǎoshī de.
content.side_f: This pen is the teacher's.
content.side_g: 笔 is a generic term covering 钢笔 (fountain pen), 铅笔 (pencil), 圆珠笔 (ballpoint). Compound verb 用笔 = 'use a pen.' Note the idiom 笔友 (pen-pal) and 笔记 (notes).

content.name: measure_flat
content.side_a: (measure word for flat objects, paper, pictures, etc.)
content.side_e: qǐng gěi wǒ yì zhāng zhǐ.
content.side_f: Please give me a sheet of paper.
content.side_g: 张 is the measure word for flat objects: paper, tickets, beds, tables, photos, maps (一张桌子, 一张票). Also used for mouths/faces (一张脸). Tone is 1st; do not confuse with surname 张.

content.name: paper
content.side_e: zhè zhāng zhǐ shàng yǒu hěn duō zì.
content.side_f: There are many characters on this piece of paper.
content.side_g: 纸 is an uncountable-style noun; always quantified with 张. Compounds: 报纸 (newspaper), 纸条 (note), 白纸 (blank paper). Not to be confused with 只 zhī (measure word / only).

content.name: teach
content.side_e: Wáng lǎoshī jiāo wǒmen Zhōngwén.
content.side_f: Teacher Wang teaches us Chinese.
content.side_g: 教 takes two objects: 教 + person + subject (教我们中文). Pronounced jiāo (1st tone) as a verb; jiào (4th tone) in compounds like 教室, 教授, 教育. Contrast with 学 (learn).

content.name: how_how_come
content.side_e: nǐ zěnme bù lái shàngkè?
content.side_f: How come you're not coming to class?
content.side_g: 怎么 asks manner/method ('how'): 怎么写? or reason ('how come,' implying surprise/mild blame): 怎么还不来? Distinguish from 为什么 (neutral 'why'). Before a verb without 了, usually means 'how'; with 了 at end, often 'what happened.'

content.name: understand
content.side_e: lǎoshī shuō de huà wǒ dōu dǒng.
content.side_f: I understand everything the teacher says.
content.side_g: 懂 means to comprehend, often as a resultative complement: 听懂 (understand by listening), 看懂 (understand by reading). Negative: 听不懂 / 看不懂. Unlike 知道 (know a fact), 懂 implies grasping meaning.

content.name: really
content.side_e: Zhōngwén yǔfǎ zhēn nán.
content.side_f: Chinese grammar is really hard.
content.side_g: 真 is an intensifying adverb expressing genuine feeling ('really, truly'), stronger and more emotive than 很. Often in exclamations: 真好! 真的吗? Do not use 真 for neutral descriptive statements — use 很 there.

content.name: where
content.side_e: nǐ jiā zài nǎli?
content.side_f: Where is your home?
content.side_g: 哪里 is interchangeable with 哪儿 (Beijing/northern colloquial). 哪里哪里 is also a modest reply to compliments ('not at all, you flatter me'). Toneless 里 here; do not confuse with 那里 (there).

content.name: preview
content.side_e: shàngkè yǐqián wǒ yùxí shēngcí.
content.side_f: Before class I preview the new words.
content.side_g: 预习 means studying ahead of class; antonym 复习 (review after). Typical objects: 课文, 生词, 语法. The 预 prefix ('in advance') also appears in 预报, 预订.

content.name: study_learn
content.side_e: wǒ xué Zhōngwén xué le liǎng nián.
content.side_f: I've been studying Chinese for two years.
content.side_g: 学 is a single-character verb; the disyllabic form 学习 is more formal/abstract. For 'study at a school,' use 学 + subject (学中文) or 上学 (attend school). Note duration: verb must repeat with duration — 学中文学了两年.

content.name: ordinal_prefix
content.side_e: zhè shì dì yī kè.
content.side_f: This is Lesson One. (lit. 'ordinal-one lesson')
content.side_g: 第 prefixes numbers to form ordinals: 第一, 第二, 第三. Always followed by a numeral + measure word/noun. Without 第, bare numbers usually mean cardinals. In dates/rankings 第 is obligatory for 'Nth.'

content.name: grammar
content.side_e: zhōngwén yǔfǎ bù róngyì.
content.side_f: Chinese grammar is not easy.
content.side_g: 语法 is a neutral, textbook term for grammar; 文法 is a Taiwan-leaning synonym. Collocations: 学语法, 讲语法, 语法规则. Do not confuse with 句子 (sentence) or 词语 (vocabulary).

content.name: easy
content.side_e: xiě zhège zì hěn róngyì.
content.side_f: Writing this character is very easy.
content.side_g: 容易 is a disyllabic adjective; its antonym is 难. Often pairs with verbs: 容易学, 容易懂. For 'easy to do,' use 很容易 + V. Avoid preceding it with 很 when paired with 不: 不容易.

content.name: new_words
content.side_e: zhè kè de shēngcí bù duō.
content.side_f: This lesson doesn't have many new words. (lit. 'new-words not many')
content.side_g: 生词 literally 'unfamiliar words,' refers to new vocabulary in a lesson; contrast 生字 (new characters). Typical collocations: 记生词, 复习生词, 预习生词. 生 here means 'unfamiliar, raw.'

content.name: many_much
content.side_e: zhè kè de shēngcí hěn duō.
content.side_f: This lesson has a lot of new words.
content.side_g: 多 as a predicate adjective requires 很 or another modifier in affirmative statements: 生词很多 (not 生词多). As a pre-nominal modifier it takes 很多 + noun (很多学生). Antonym: 少.
